cards.meaning: once; in the event that (a consequential condition is triggered)
cards.example: 一旦霍尔木兹海峡关闭，油价就会飙升。
cards.examplePinyin: yīdàn Huò'ěrmùzī hǎixiá guānbì, yóujià jiù huì biāoshēng
cards.exampleMeaning: Once the Strait of Hormuz closes, oil prices will spike.
cards.note: HSK-4 advanced connective; register leans 书面语/analytical — a natural fit for risk and policy language. Signature pattern: 一旦…就… ('once X, then Y'). Near-synonym contrast vs 如果 (rúguǒ, 'if'): 如果 is a neutral hypothetical; 一旦 is stronger and pointed — a consequential trigger you're actively bracing for, not just any possibility.
cards.tiesTo: the Hormuz 'unnavigable' declaration and the oil-shock risk running through the whole show

cards.meaning: rather than X, better to Y (weigh two options, pick the better)
cards.example: 与其盲目扩张，不如控制债务。
cards.examplePinyin: yǔqí mángmù kuòzhāng, bùrú kòngzhì zhàiwù
cards.exampleMeaning: Rather than expand blindly, it's better to control debt.
cards.note: HSK-4 comparative-preference frame, formal/argumentative register — a finance-desk staple. Collocation: 与其 A，不如 B, where B is the recommended choice. Near-synonym contrast vs 宁可 (nìngkě, 'would rather, even at a cost'): 宁可 stresses willingness to accept a downside; 与其…不如 simply compares two paths and endorses the better one.
cards.tiesTo: Oracle's capex-vs-debt question and the market's AI-capex-sustainability doubt

cards.example: Pabagsak ang merkado ng mga chip kahapon.
cards.exampleMeaning: The chip market was sliding yesterday.
cards.note: From bagsak (to fall). Everyday news-Tagalog directional word; the opposite is pataas (rising). Collocation: pabagsak na presyo / pabagsak na merkado (a falling price / a falling market). Stronger and more visual than bumababa (merely 'going down') — pabagsak carries the sense of a decisive drop.
cards.tiesTo: the semiconductor rout and Korea's KOSPI circuit-breaker session

cards.meaning: an obstacle, hindrance, or bottleneck that slows you down
cards.example: Ang kakulangan ng gas turbine ay isang malaking sagabal sa pagtatayo ng mga data center.
cards.exampleMeaning: The gas-turbine shortage is a big bottleneck to building data centers.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance vs hadlang (both translate loosely as 'obstacle'): sagabal is friction that slows you — you can still move; hadlang blocks you completely — you stop. So a turbine backlog is a sagabal (drag), not yet a hadlang (a wall). Slightly formal but common in the news.
cards.tiesTo: gas-turbine backlogs as the binding constraint on the data-center buildout
